evals.prompt:
Post the research findings to GitHub Discussions. The artifact is at /tmp/report.md, targeting the repo acme/research. The report covers AI adoption trends in enterprise software.

Contents of /tmp/report.md:
# AI Adoption in Enterprise Software

This report examines adoption trends across Fortune 500 firms. Key findings: 73% of enterprises piloting LLMs by Q4 2025, primary barrier is data governance, and open-source models closing the gap on proprietary alternatives.

## Highlights
- LLM adoption: 73% piloting
- Top barrier: data governance
- OSS momentum accelerating

Published: https://acme.github.io/research/ai-adoption-2025
evals.expected_output: The skill checks for gh CLI and auth, resolves the repo and discussion category via GraphQL, composes an announce-type discussion body from the artifact summary (no internal urn:mif: references), escapes the body with jq, and posts via a createDiscussion GraphQL mutation. Reports the resulting discussion URL.

deterministic_checks.description: Dependency check: gh CLI presence is tested before any other work
Auth check runs as part of the dependency gate
Publish phase uses the createDiscussion GraphQL mutation
Body is escaped with jq -Rs before being passed to GraphQL
No urn:mif: finding IDs leak into the transcript output (body or report)

expectations.description: Dependency check runs before any GraphQL or gh command
expectations.criteria: The 'command -v gh' or equivalent check appears before any 'gh api' call in the transcript

expectations.description: The composed discussion body includes the artifact title and at least one highlight from the artifact, without enumerating internal data files
expectations.criteria: The body shown or posted contains 'AI Adoption' and at least one bullet matching a highlight from the artifact

expectations.description: The skill reports a discussion URL or the dry-run equivalent at the end
expectations.criteria: Final output includes a GitHub discussion URL or explicit dry-run summary

evals.prompt: Post the research to GitHub Discussions but gh is not installed on this machine. Artifact is at /tmp/report.md. Repo: acme/research.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that gh is not installed, prints a human-readable message explaining the channel is unavailable and how to install gh, then exits cleanly without an error code. No GraphQL calls are made.

deterministic_checks.description: The dependency check for gh CLI is performed
Output contains a user-facing message about gh not being installed
No createDiscussion mutation is attempted when gh is absent
No urn:mif: IDs leak in the degraded output

expectations.description: Skill degrades gracefully — no exception or error traceback, no panic, just a clear user-facing explanation
expectations.criteria: The message is informational (mentions install instructions or 'gh auth login') and the skill does not raise an unhandled error or leave partial state

expectations.description: Skill does not attempt to proceed past the dependency check
expectations.criteria: There is no GraphQL API call, no category resolution, and no body composition after the failed dependency check

evals.prompt:
Dry-run posting the AI adoption report to GitHub Discussions. Use: artifact /tmp/report.md, repo acme/research, type announce, --dry-run flag.

Contents of /tmp/report.md:
# AI Adoption in Enterprise Software

Key finding: 73% of Fortune 500 firms are piloting LLMs. Top barrier: data governance.

Published: https://acme.github.io/research/ai-adoption-2025
evals.expected_output: The skill performs the dependency check and category resolution, composes the discussion body, then prints the composed title, category, and body to stdout and stops — without calling createDiscussion. The printed body must not contain any urn:mif: finding references.

deterministic_checks.description: Dependency check still runs in dry-run mode
createDiscussion mutation is NOT invoked in dry-run mode
Output explicitly signals dry-run mode to the user
No urn:mif: finding IDs appear in the dry-run body output

expectations.description: The dry-run output shows the composed discussion body derived from the artifact, including the title and at least one highlight
expectations.criteria: Printed body contains 'AI Adoption' (from artifact title) and at least one bullet or sentence drawn from the artifact content

expectations.description: The dry-run output shows the resolved category and repository
expectations.criteria: Output mentions the resolved category name (e.g. Announcements, General) and 'acme/research'

evals.prompt: Announce the research to GitHub Discussions. No artifact file is provided. Repo: acme/research, type: announce.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that no artifact was supplied for an announce-type post and emits the specific error: 'No artifact to announce. Render the report first.' It does not proceed to Phase 1 or GraphQL calls.

deterministic_checks.description: The exact error text from SKILL.md is emitted when artifact is missing for announce type
No publish attempt is made when the artifact is missing
Category resolution GraphQL is not called when artifact is missing

expectations.description: Error message directs the user to render the report first, matching the SKILL.md-specified text
expectations.criteria: Output contains a message closely matching 'No artifact to announce. Render the report first.' — exact phrasing or very close paraphrase
